follow_up: Have a manager Codex session register itself via `workerctl register-manager --name <MGR_NAME> --pid <MGR_PID> --cwd <CWD>`.
Create a task and bind the pair: `workerctl tasks --create <TASK> --goal "<goal>"` then `workerctl bind --task <TASK> --worker <NAME> --manager <MGR_NAME>`.
The manager Codex drives the supervision loop by calling `workerctl cycle <TASK>` repeatedly and reading the returned JSON.
why_or_why_not: This Codex session cannot register itself as a tmux-backed worker. Failed checks: workerctl_on_path. A Codex session running outside tmux can still register itself as a manager via `workerctl register-manager`.
